source: Extracted from the production cover/design system (build_design_pdf.py \definecolor set). This is the cyberpunk-noir identity used across the SPZ books and the Neko Death Cult audio drama.
mood: industrial-goth cyberpunk noir — near-black void, hot neon, cold signal, newsprint grit

void.role: primary background / ground. Near-black, not pure black. The default field everything sits on.
void.usage: page backgrounds, cover fields, dark UI

pink.role: primary accent — hot magenta. The brand's loudest color; the punk shout, the title glow.
pink.usage: titles, primary CTAs, highlights, the SPZ logotype flush

cyan.role: secondary accent — cold signal cyan. The 'signal' half of 'signal finds signal'; the terminal glow.
cyan.usage: links, code/terminal accents, secondary highlights, glitch lines

paper.role: primary foreground text on void; a dirty off-white, newsprint not paper-white.

muted.role: muted slate — de-emphasis, metadata, rules, secondary text.

pairings.never: do not put pink on cyan or cyan on pink — both are max-chroma neon and vibrate. Separate them with void or paper.

notes: Two neons, one void. The pink/cyan tension IS the brand — hot vs cold, flesh vs signal. Use void generously between them.
Backgrounds are #030303, never #000000 — the near-black keeps a hair of warmth and lets true-black shadows read.
Newsprint/VHS grit texture over the void is on-brand (the tabloid cover lineage).
For light/print contexts, invert: void text on paper field, pink + cyan kept as accents.
